name: I slipped at work in Salt Lake City yesterday but didn't tell anyone yet, what do I do right now?
description: Report immediately in writing to your employer and seek medical care—Utah requires notice within 180 days or you lose benefits.[1]

mainEntity.name: I slipped at work in Salt Lake City yesterday but didn't tell anyone yet, what do I do right now?

acceptedAnswer.text: Report immediately in writing to your employer and seek medical care—Utah requires notice within 180 days or you lose benefits.[1]
acceptedAnswer.description: **Report the injury to your employer immediately, ideally in writing, to preserve your claim.** Utah law requires reporting within 180 days, but delays can jeopardize benefits; employers must notify their insurer within 7 days.[1][2] Key factors: Document how, when, and where it happened; seek medical care and inform the doctor it's work-related; get copies of Form 122 and Form 100 from your employer. Go to a doctor promptly, avoid giving recorded statements to insurers without advice, and consider consulting a workers' comp specialist if issues arise. Protecting your rights starts with quick, documented action.
